nodes.context: Collection of criminal organizations of Moroccan descent active in Europe, specializing in cocaine and synthetic drug trafficking.
Criminal group that stole a large cocaine shipment in Antwerp in 2012, triggering the Mocro-War.
Moroccan Jewish crime family considered part of the Israeli mafia, not included in the Moroccan mafia.
Moroccan Jewish crime family considered part of the Israeli mafia.
Moroccan Jewish crime family considered part of the Israeli mafia.
Criminal organizations including Moroccan Jewish clans, collaborated with Mocro Maffia in synthetic drug distribution.
Term for Moroccan criminal networks active in Antwerp, especially in Borgerhout district.
Dutch criminal syndicates that inspired Moroccan communities to enter hard drug business in the 1980s.
Collection of criminal organizations of Moroccan descent, specializing in drug trafficking in Europe.
International cocaine cartel led by Ridouan Taghi, controlling a third of Europe's cocaine trade.
Italian mafia-type organization involved in drug trafficking and arms dealing.
Irish organized crime groups active in Europe, involved in drug trafficking.
Serbian organized crime groups involved in drug trafficking in Europe.
Albanian organized crime groups involved in drug trafficking in Europe.
Moroccan drug clan involved in cocaine trafficking, exposed by Sky ECC decryption.
Colombian criminal organization that provided protection to Saïd Razzouki.
Colombian drug cartel that provided protection to Saïd Razzouki.
Local crime family allied with Taghi, later turned rival after accidental killing.
South American drug cartels that supply cocaine to Moroccan networks.
Mexican drug cartels that have privileged relationships with Moroccan mafia.
Chilean cartel boss who had a close friendship with Ridouan Taghi.
Rival drug lord assassinated in 2014, allowing Taghi to rise.
Rival drug lord assassinated in 2014, allowing Taghi to rise.
Camorra drugs and arms dealer, part of super cartel with Taghi.
Irish reputed gang boss, part of super cartel with Taghi.
Bosnian drug trafficker, part of super cartel with Taghi.
Criminal organization led by Samir Bouyakhrichan, involved in large-scale drug trafficking with Colombian cartels.
Criminal organization founded by Naoufal Fassih, involved in assassinations and drug trafficking.
Criminal organization led by Houssine Ait Soussan, distributing cocaine across Western Europe.
Moroccan criminal organization allied with the Turtle clan in Antwerp.
Irish organized crime group involved in drug trafficking and money laundering.
Criminal organization involved in the assassination of Samir Bouyakhrichan.
Moroccan crime family led by brothers Mohamed A., Rachid A., and Hassan A., controlling cocaine trade in Flanders.
Belgian-Moroccan drug trafficking group dismantled in 2015 after seizure of 3.4 tonnes of cocaine in Antwerp.
Criminal organization led by Ridouan Taghi, targeted in the Marengo trial, involved in drug trafficking and murders.
Iranian dissident group, member Mohammad Reza Kolahi Samadi was assassinated in the Netherlands.

edges.context: The Turtle clan's theft of a cocaine shipment in 2012 sparked the Mocro-War, a conflict involving Mocro Maffia groups.

Dutch Penose syndicates inspired and cooperated with Moroccan criminal networks in the hard drug trade.
Borgerokko-Maffia is a local term for Moroccan criminal networks in Antwerp, which are part of the broader Mocro Maffia.

Moroccan mafia has privileged relationships with Mexican cartels.
